channel.title: NYT > Style
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: At the ‘Euphoria’ Wedding, All Eyes Were on the Guests
item.description: At Cassie and Nate’s nuptials, guests arrived to steal the show, and that was the point. Natasha Newman-Thomas, the show’s costume designer, used attention-grabbing looks to reflect each character’s evolution.
item.dc:creator: Sadiba Hasan

item.title: Armani Goes Back to the Archive
item.description: A new collection dusts off decades-old creations, plus a dispatch from a Brunello Cucinelli movie premiere and the Political Tie Tracker.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Gallagher

item.title: A Rabbi Talks Torah on TikTok
item.description: Rabbi Shalom Landau has found a large new audience online, some of which isn’t Jewish. He sticks to ancient wisdom and hopes for the best.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Bernstein
item.media:credit: Jonah Markowitz for The New York Times
item.media:description: Rabbi Shalom Landau at his desk in the Williamsburg neighborhood of Brooklyn.

item.title: How the Brothers Behind Manhattan’s Aicon Art Gallery Found Themselves in Infinite Feuds
item.description: For a decade and a half, Prajit and Projjal Dutta have led the market for South Asian art in the United States. Now they rarely speak to each other, except through lawyers.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Bernstein
item.media:credit: Vincent Tullo for The New York Times

item.title: How Tim Cook’s Tech Uniform Helped Make Apple Trillions
item.description: The unassuming look of the outgoing Apple chief executive was an asset, as he navigated pop culture, the president and following Steve Jobs.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Gallagher
item.media:credit: Jim Wilson/The New York Times
item.media:description: In his navy polos and dark jeans, Mr. Cook became a recognizable but unassuming figurehead of Apple. It may have been an asset for him to present as a kind of blank slate.

item.title: How ‘The Devil Wears Prada 2’ Red Carpet Looks Came Together
item.description: The press tour for “The Devil Wears Prada 2” has been a style spectacle for its stars and their stylists, who have relied on group chats, sweatpants and camaraderie to pull it off.
item.dc:creator: Yola Mzizi
item.media:credit: Mike Coppola/Getty Images

item.title: Meryl Streep, Anne Hathaway and More Red Carpet Looks from “The Devil Wears Prada 2”
item.description: At a starry premiere at Lincoln Center, fashion industry figures reflected on just how much the original film got right.
item.dc:creator: Callie Holtermann and Lanna Apisukh
item.media:description: Stars of the “The Devil Wears Prada 2,” Meryl Streep, Emily Blunt, Stanley Tucci and Anne Hathaway, pose at the film’s premiere at Lincoln Center in Manhattan.

item.title: The Girl With the Sunglasses Tattoo
item.description: My daughter left me way too young, so I find ways to keep her with me.
item.dc:creator: Lindzi Scharf
item.media:credit: Brian Rea

item.title: A Wedding That Included a Mister and ‘The Miz’
item.description: Madison Greco and Steven Lynch bonded over their love of professional wrestling. The bride’s favorite wrestler led their wedding ceremony in Las Vegas.
item.dc:creator: Sadiba Hasan

item.title: Turning a House Into a Home — and a Wedding Venue, Too
item.description: Matthew Quinn and Blair Carnahan took on redesigning their new home in just four months. Then they took on another major project: planning their nuptials there.
item.dc:creator: Jenny Block

item.title: Two Brooklynites Find Love on Reddit
item.description: After connecting through an online post, Giuliana Cerullo and Abhipsit Srivastava met face-to-face and went on to build a relationship grounded in shared rituals that brought them closer.
item.dc:creator: Abby McCarter

item.title: By the End of a Hiking Trip, Their Relationship Was Elevated
item.description: Alyssa Levine and Liam Gallagher, both doctors, met through competitive swimming in 2010. A decade later, they felt sparks on Mount Shasta.
item.dc:creator: Louise Rafkin

item.title: King Charles and Queen Camilla Hit the Red Carpet at N.Y.C. Gala
item.description: Anna Wintour, Martha Stewart and others greeted the royal couple at the King’s Trust Gala, during their official state visit to the United States.
item.dc:creator: Remy Tumin
item.category: Charles III, King of the United Kingdom
Camilla, Queen Consort of the United Kingdom
item.media:credit: Mike Segar/Reuters
item.media:description: King Charles and Queen Camilla are received by Bonnie Brennan, the chief executive of Christie’s, at the fifth annual King’s Trust Global Gala in New York City on Wednesday.

item.title: Top 10 Reasons We Love the Original ‘Devil Wears Prada’ Movie
item.description: The 2006 comedy is filled with moments big and small that have stood the test of time, thanks in no small part to Meryl Streep’s turn as Miranda Priestly.
item.dc:creator: Maya Phillips

item.title: Michaela Jaé Rodriguez’s Necklace Gives her Peace
item.description: The peace sign necklace, imbued with familial significance, is lent to her by the actress’s mother, only when the occasion calls for it.
item.dc:creator: Amelia Diamond
item.media:credit: Dolly Faibyshev for The New York Times

item.title: Palantir Is Making a French Chore Coat. Yes, That Palantir.
item.description: The data analysis company is selling a version of the jacket that it says will show its commitment to “re-industrializing America.”
item.dc:creator: Yola Mzizi
item.media:credit: Palantir
item.media:description: Palantir’s new chore coat comes in bright blue and black.

item.title: With the Arrival of Goop Kitchen, Is New York Food Going Full L.A.?
item.description: Gwyneth Paltrow’s healthy-eating enterprise is just one of several recent Los Angeles exports to a rival city with its own ideas about what’s good.
item.dc:creator: Julia Moskin
item.media:credit: Emma McIntyre/Getty Images
item.media:description: The mission of Goop Kitchen, according to Gwyneth Paltrow, is to bring healthy food to a mass audience. Last week, New York City became its first market outside California.

item.title: The Good List: 6 Things to Add Some Joy to Your Day
item.description: Showering at the airport, blossoms in seams and the bar for joy.
item.dc:creator: Melissa Kirsch

item.title: Tiny Love Stories: ‘Everyone Was a Few Drinks In’
item.description: Modern Love in miniature, featuring reader-submitted stories of no more than 100 words.
item.media:credit: Brian Rea

item.title: New Yorkers and European World Cup Fans Debate Walking to MetLife in New Jersey
item.description: Instead of paying $150 for NJ Transit tickets to get to the stadium, some Europeans online are suggesting a scenic stroll through New Jersey’s interstates and swamps.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Roy
item.media:credit: Al Bello/Getty Images
item.media:description: What started as a debate about transit and infrastructure around the World Cup this summer grew into an online tiff about American-European cultural differences.

item.title: Melania Trump, Queen Camilla and the Look of the Special Relationship
item.description: Parsing a state visit told in photo ops, and style.
item.dc:creator: Vanessa Friedman
item.category: Camilla, Queen Consort of the United Kingdom
Charles III, King of the United Kingdom
item.media:credit: Kenny Holston/The New York Times
item.media:description: In pink: Queen Camilla, King Charles III, President Trump and Melania Trump at the state dinner on Tuesday.

item.title: The New York Philharmonic Gives Gustavo Dudamel a Warm Welcome
item.description: The flamboyant conductor Gustavo Dudamel got a jubilant reception at the New York Philharmonic’s spring gala on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Bahr and Dolly Faibyshev
item.category: Lincoln Center for the Performing Arts
item.media:credit: Dolly Faibyshev for The New York Times

item.title: Can I Skip My Niece’s Graduation to Avoid the Politics?
item.description: A reader wants to be a supportive aunt at the Naval Academy commencement, but the prospect of political speeches has her gritting her teeth.
item.dc:creator: Philip Galanes
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Miguel Porlan

item.title: The Literary Journal Cake Zine Throws a Steak-Themed Party in Manhattan
item.description: The literary food magazine Cake Zine celebrated a savory special issue.
item.dc:creator: Paul McAdory and Jutharat Pinyodoonyachet
item.media:credit: Jutharat Pinyodoonyachet for The New York Times
item.media:description: A copy of Steak Zine on a table at Funny Bar on Manhattan’s Lower East Side.

item.title: A Visit to Martha Lady Sitwell’s ‘Grottage’
item.description: A visit to the “grottage” of Martha Sitwell, a breakout star of the Bravo series “Ladies of London: The New Reign.”
item.dc:creator: Jessica Roy
item.media:credit: Bella Howard for The New York Times
item.media:description: Martha Lady Sitwell has become a fan favorite on Bravo’s “Ladies of London: The New Reign.”

item.title: 5 Hotels for Your Next U.S. Road Trip
item.description: Add these new or newly revamped accommodations to your summer driving itinerary.
item.dc:creator: Stephanie Rosenbloom

item.title: Chanel Stages a Met Gala Curtain Raiser
item.description: The label’s cruise show in Biarritz, France, offered a preview of what we may see on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Vanessa Friedman
item.media:credit: Marc Piasecki/Getty Images

item.title: Fashion Can’t Get Over Michael Jackson
item.description: With a new biopic crushing at the box office, a reflection on the industry’s selective memory around MJ. Plus, cowboy Crocs.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Gallagher
item.media:credit: Paul Drinkwater/NBCUniversal, via Getty Images
item.media:description: MJ in his black loafers and shiny socks in 1983.

item.title: George Clooney Calls for Unity After White House Correspondents’ Dinner Shooting
item.description: The actor, who was honored at Film at Lincoln Center’s Chaplin Award Gala, denounced political violence after the attempted assassination of President Trump.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Bahr
item.category: Lincoln Center for the Performing Arts
item.media:credit: Krista Schlueter for The New York Times

item.title: Street Style Look of the Week: A Work Wear Staple in Gentle Pastels
item.description: A stylish woman in Upper Manhattan can’t be bothered with “tucking in shirts and all the things.”
item.dc:creator: Simbarashe Cha

item.title: Ryan Gosling’s ‘Project Hail Mary’ Sweater Is a Hit. It’s Not for Sale.
item.description: A cardigan worn by Gosling’s character in the blockbuster science-fiction movie has become something of a breakout star — if you want it, grab your knitting needles!
item.dc:creator: Alisha Haridasani Gupta

item.title: Jamie Ding’s ‘Jeopardy’ Sweaters Made Him a Style Champion
item.description: Ding’s approach to dressing, with his spectacular spectrum of sweaters, suited a contestant who remained ice-cube calm as he climbed up the show’s leaderboard.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Gallagher

item.title: Jamie Ding’s ‘Jeopardy!’ Streak Comes to an End
item.description: Jamie Ding, a self-described “faceless bureaucrat” from New Jersey, became a TV sensation during his 31-game winning streak.
item.dc:creator: Callie Holtermann
item.media:credit: Bryan Anselm for The New York Times
item.media:description: Jamie Ding is back home in Lawrenceville, N.J., after winning $880,000 on “Jeopardy!”

item.title: Bravo’s ‘Summer House’ Love Triangle and Leaked Audio Drama: What to Know
item.description: After West Wilson and Amanda Batula confirmed their relationship, fans were anticipating drama at the show’s next reunion. But audio appeared to leak weeks before its air date.
item.dc:creator: Kellina Moore
item.media:credit: Getty Images

item.title: Zoë Kravitz, Harry Styles and Why Celebrity Engagements Might Move Quickly
item.description: To the average person, celebrity relationships can appear to move at a clip. There are a number of reasons for that.
item.dc:creator: Sadiba Hasan

item.title: 7 ‘Body Types’ in the Met’s ‘Costume Art’ Fashion Exhibition
item.description: The museum’s top fashion curator offers an up-close look at garments featured in the show, which argues that clothing is a connective thread throughout art.
item.dc:creator: Callie Holtermann and Vincent Tullo
item.media:credit: Photographs by Vincent Tullo for The New York Times

item.title: After the Gunfire, the Parties Went On, Uneasily
item.description: Hosts in the nation’s capital decided to carry on in the wake of a violent incident at the White House correspondents’ dinner.
item.dc:creator: Callie Holtermann and Andrew Trunsky
item.media:credit: Pete Kiehart for The New York Times
item.media:description: Guests at a party hosted by the newsletter platform Substack on Saturday night reacting to news of gunfire at the White House correspondents’ dinner.

item.title: Why Fashion Designer Elie Saab Will Never Leave Lebanon, Despite the War
item.description: The designer and his son discuss making couture during a bombing and the reason it matters.
item.dc:creator: Vanessa Friedman
item.media:credit: Swan Gallet/Getty Images
item.media:description: Elie Saab and Elie Saab Jr. are united in their commitment to work through war.

item.title: Can the Reinvented Delano Hotel Resuscitate South Beach?
item.description: Once a magnet for the fashion and music sets of the 1990s, the hotel has been rebranded as a wellness-leaning space with nonalcoholic cocktails and a spa with a communal sauna.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Felder
item.media:credit: Scott McIntyre for The New York Times
item.media:description: The South Beach stretch of Collins Avenue that includes the Delano.

item.title: Queen Elizabeth II’s Off-Duty Wardrobe Goes on View at Buckingham Palace
item.description: A new exhibition takes a closer look at Queen Elizabeth II’s wardrobe of boots, barn coats, head scarves and tartan skirts.
item.dc:creator: Yola Mzizi
item.category: Elizabeth II, Queen of Great Britain
item.media:credit: Tim Graham/Hulton Archive, via Getty Images
item.media:description: Head scarves, like the one Queen Elizabeth II wore to the Royal Windsor Horse Show in May 1968, were a pillar of her off-duty style.

item.title: She Found Out She Was Pregnant Just Before He Revealed an Affair
item.description: Rachel Sanders eventually came to terms with Jason Miller’s confession that he was involved with a mutual male friend. “I lost my marriage,” she said, “but I accepted Jason.”
item.dc:creator: Louise Rafkin

item.title: Make Way for the Investment Bank Influencers
item.description: Zoomers in finance are posting day-in-the-life videos on TikTok, alarming compliance officers. Should Wall Street reconsider its social media policies?
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark

item.title: Aldwyth, Ascetic Whose Artwork Reordered the World, Dies at 90
item.description: Holding to an austere lifestyle on a South Carolina island, she made collages and Joseph Cornell-like contraptions that told stories about art history and her place in it, and outside of it.
item.dc:creator: Penelope Green

item.title: N.F.L. Style Will Never Beat N.B.A. Style
item.description: Plus: some great Japanese designers and a final thought on the press tour for “The Devil Wears Prada 2.”
item.dc:creator: Jacob Gallagher
item.media:credit: Kirby Lee/Imagn Images, via Reuters Connect

item.title: Inside the Time 100 Gala: Where Victoria and David Beckham Dine with MrBeast
item.description: Titans of influence filled the Deutsche Bank Center in Manhattan on Thursday night for an evening of selfies and self-congratulation.
item.dc:creator: Madison Malone Kircher and Nina Westervelt
item.media:credit: Nina Westervelt for The New York Times
